title: Kniphofia bruceae
intro: Kniphofia bruceae, also known as the red-hot poker, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asphodelaceae. The plant is known for its vibrant red and yellow flowers, which have been used in traditional medicine for their health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Kniphofia bruceae include kaempferol, quercetin, and isorhapontigenin, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Kniphofia bruceae is often prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being used to make a tincture or extract, which are used to treat a variety of health conditions.
taxonomy: Kniphofia bruceae is a species of flowering plant in the family Asphodelaceae, commonly known as the asphodel family. It is native to the mountains of Lesotho and South Africa. The genus Kniphofia is a group of plants that are often referred to as red-hot poker plants due to their brightly colored flowers. Kniphofia bruceae is a perennial plant that produces tall spikes of tubular flowers in shades of orange and yellow. The plant is classified as a succulent and is known for its ability to thrive in dry conditions.
